1: Moreover the LORD LORD answered Job, and said,
2: He that reproveth God, let him answer it.
3: Then Job answered the LORD LORD , and said,
4: I will lay mine hand upon my mouth.
5: Yea, twice; but I will proceed no further.
6: Then answered the LORD LORD unto Job out of the whirlwind, and said,
7: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
8: Wilt thou condemn me, that thou mayest be righteous?
9: Or canst thou thunder with a voice like him?
10: And array thyself with glory and beauty.
11: And behold every one that is that is proud, and abase him.
12: And tread down the wicked in their place.
13: And And bind their faces in secret.
14: That thine own right hand can save thee.
15: He eateth grass as an ox.
16: And his force is is in the navel of his belly.
17: The sinews of his stones are wrapped together.
18: His bones are are like bars of iron.
19: He that made him can make his sword to approach unto him unto him .
20: Where all the beasts of the field play.
21: In the covert of the reed, and fens.
22: The willows of the brook compass him about.
23: He trusteth that he can draw up Jordan into his mouth.
24: His His nose pierceth through snares.
